response.justification: While Bogdan is a seasoned software engineer with strong distributed-systems and event-driven architecture experience, his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ is the core technical requirement and appears only briefly — six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis project — and is notably absent from his skills list. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript for web and SaaS products. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, industrial communication protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or familiarity with IEC 62443 secure-development standards, all of which are central to the position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience including high-performance, low-latency micro-services (Dunnhumby) and event-driven/distributed architecture (GISCollective)
Demonstrated exposure to hardware and embedded contexts (robotic arms at Altom, NI LabWindows C/C++ components, wave-field-synthesis thesis in C++)
Hands-on experience with REST APIs, Node.js back-end systems, React, and agile practices — covering several of the role's advantageous skills
concerns: C++ depth is minimal and stale: the only substantive C++ role was a 6-month stint in 2014–2015; it is not listed among his self-reported skills
No evidence of industrial-automation domain expertise, IEC 62443 / SDL security practices, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT in an IoT context)
Career trajectory is firmly in web/SaaS full-stack development, not in on-premises embedded or OT-platform engineering required by this role

key_factors.factor: No industrial/OT domain or security-standards experience
